# Alpha Attachment Report

This report measures block attachment distances under the fixed metric:

$$d_\alpha((x,y),(x',y'))=\sqrt{\alpha^2(x-x')^2+(y-y')^2}.$$

Reading guide:

Small minimum distances across alpha values indicate that each new block touches the previous support robustly, not only because of a particular axis scaling. Mean and maximum distances describe the spread of genuinely new regions under the chosen metric.
